paper_title: NTL-amplified cryogenic light detectors with optically transparent electrodes

P000_S0010.reason: The perturbed table swaps all ITO and Au bonding pad values, contradicting the original table in surrounding_context where ITO has Ar flux 16, pressure 8.8e-3, Power 40W, etc.

P001_S0011.quote: maintained at $8.8 \times 10^{-3}$~mbar with high-purity argon (99.999\%) supplied as sputtering gas at a constant flow rate of 16~sccm
P001_S0011.reason: The related_passages table shows ITO electrodes use Ar flux 16 sccm and pressure 8.8e-3 mbar, but those are the Au bonding pad values; the ITO procedure text now contradicts the table by using Au pad parameters instead of ITO parameters (17 sccm, 9.6e-3 mbar, 28 nm/min).
P001_S0011.original:
ITO films were deposited by DC magnetron sputtering using a 4-inch balanced planar magnetron cathode equipped with a commercial 4N purity ITO target (90~wt.\% In$_2$O$_3$ -- 10~wt.\% SnO$_2$).
The working pressure during deposition was maintained at $9.6 \times 10^{-3}$~mbar with high-purity argon (99.999\%) supplied as sputtering gas at a constant flow rate of 17~sccm; no additional oxygen was introduced into the plasma.
All depositions were carried out at room temperature without intentional substrate heating.
Under these conditions, we measured an approximate deposition rate of 28~nm\,min$^{-1}$. This value is determined measuring ex-situ the thickness of a test deposition with a contact profilometer, and diving this quantity by the deposition time.
P001_S0011.perturbed:
ITO films were deposited by DC magnetron sputtering using a 4-inch balanced planar magnetron cathode equipped with a commercial 4N purity ITO target (90~wt.\% In$_2$O$_3$ -- 10~wt.\% SnO$_2$).
The working pressure during deposition was maintained at $8.8 \times 10^{-3}$~mbar with high-purity argon (99.999\%) supplied as sputtering gas at a constant flow rate of 16~sccm; no additional oxygen was introduced into the plasma.
All depositions were carried out at room temperature without intentional substrate heating.
Under these conditions, we measured an approximate deposition rate of 30~nm\,min$^{-1}$. This value is determined measuring ex-situ the thickness of a test deposition with a contact profilometer, and diving this quantity by the deposition time.

P002_S0012.quote: performed at a power of 100 W under an argon working pressure of $9.6 \times 10^{-3}$~mbar
P002_S0012.reason: The related_passages table shows Au bonding pad uses Power 100W and pressure 9.6e-3 mbar, but the gold pad deposition section in related_passages explicitly states 'power of 40 W under an argon working pressure of 8.8e-3 mbar', contradicting the perturbed values.
P002_S0012.original:
Gold contact pads were deposited for device fabrication using a similar magnetron sputtering system equipped with a 2-inch gold target. The deposition was performed at a power of 40 W under an argon working pressure of $8.8 \times 10^{-3}$~mbar.
A gold thickness of 300 nm was deposited without the use of any adhesion layer (e.g., Ti or Cr). The absence of an intermediate layer was chosen to avoid potential interface diffusion or additional stress contributions that could affect cryogenic performance.
P002_S0012.perturbed:
Gold contact pads were deposited for device fabrication using a similar magnetron sputtering system equipped with a 2-inch gold target. The deposition was performed at a power of 100 W under an argon working pressure of $9.6 \times 10^{-3}$~mbar.
A gold thickness of 300 nm was deposited without the use of any adhesion layer (e.g., Ti or Cr). The absence of an intermediate layer was chosen to avoid potential interface diffusion or additional stress contributions that could affect cryogenic performance.

P003_S0013.reason: The surrounding_context and related_passages clearly show this section discusses optical/reflectance measurements of ITO coatings, not thermal characterization, and the label remains 'optical characterization' creating an internal inconsistency.

P004_S0014.quote: no difference is expected, since the samples show a perfectly uniform color
P004_S0014.reason: The surrounding_context original states 'a difference is expected, since the samples show a radial color change', and the figure caption in surrounding_context confirms 'some variation, as expected from the visible diffraction patterns', directly contradicting the perturbed claim.
P004_S0014.original:
For this production, the ITO thickness was targeted to absorb photons at the peak value of lithium molybdate emission (550-600 nm), the scintillator chosen for the CUPID experiment \cite{Cupid:2025avs}. 
To verify that the target was met, we performed reflectance measurements of the ITO coatings deposited on both sides of the silicon wafer.
These measurements were performed at room temperature, probing both the center of and the edge of the coating with a spectrophotometer (Konica Minolta and CM-2600d). By performing the measurements at two different positions, deposition non-uniformity associated with the magnetron sputtering source can be evaluated. From the visual inspection of the samples shown in Figure~\ref{fig:devices}, a difference is expected, since the samples show a radial color change.
P004_S0014.perturbed:
For this production, the ITO thickness was targeted to absorb photons at the peak value of lithium molybdate emission (550-600 nm), the scintillator chosen for the CUPID experiment \cite{Cupid:2025avs}. 
To verify that the target was met, we performed reflectance measurements of the ITO coatings deposited on both sides of the silicon wafer.
These measurements were performed at room temperature, probing both the center of and the edge of the coating with a spectrophotometer (Konica Minolta and CM-2600d). By performing the measurements at two different positions, deposition non-uniformity associated with the magnetron sputtering source can be evaluated. From the visual inspection of the samples shown in Figure~\ref{fig:devices}, no difference is expected, since the samples show a perfectly uniform color.

P005_S0015.quote: designed to maximize the reflection of light in the 500-600~nm range
P005_S0015.reason: The surrounding_context original caption states electrodes were designed to 'avoid the reflection of light in the 500-600 nm range', and the following text confirms ITO4 shows 'a minimum in the 500-600 nm region', directly contradicting 'maximize the reflection'.

P006_S0016.quote: for both ITO2 and ITO3 samples
P006_S0016.reason: The surrounding_context and related_passages consistently refer to ITO1 and ITO4 as the tested devices, and the immediately following text discusses 'ITO4 shows' and 'ITO1 presents', contradicting the claim that ITO2 and ITO3 are reported.
P006_S0016.original: The results of these measurements are reported in Figure~\ref{fig:reflectivity} for both ITO1 and ITO4 samples.
P006_S0016.perturbed: The results of these measurements are reported in Figure~\ref{fig:reflectivity} for both ITO2 and ITO3 samples.

P007_S0017.quote: ITO1 shows the expected reflectivity spectrum, with a minimum in the 500-600~nm region. On the other hand, ITO4 presents a reflectivity spectrum with two peaks
P007_S0017.reason: The surrounding_context original states the opposite: ITO4 shows the minimum (expected thin-film behavior) and ITO1 shows two peaks (thick sample behavior), which is also consistent with the figure caption and thickness calculation formulas.
P007_S0017.original: ITO4 shows the expected reflectivity spectrum, with a minimum in the 500-600~nm region. On the other hand, ITO1 presents a reflectivity spectrum with two peaks typical of thick samples.
P007_S0017.perturbed: ITO1 shows the expected reflectivity spectrum, with a minimum in the 500-600~nm region. On the other hand, ITO4 presents a reflectivity spectrum with two peaks typical of thick samples.

P008_S0018.quote: ITO1 shows the highest uniformity, as expected from its appearance
P008_S0018.reason: The surrounding_context original states 'ITO1 shows higher variations, as expected from its appearance', and the figure caption confirms 'some variation, as expected from the visible diffraction patterns', directly contradicting the claim of highest uniformity.
P008_S0018.original: The depositions on the two sides of both silicon wafers have similar responses, as well as the corner and central regions. ITO1 shows higher variations, as expected from its appearance.
P008_S0018.perturbed: The depositions on the two sides of both silicon wafers have identical responses, showing no variation between the corner and central regions. ITO1 shows the highest uniformity, as expected from its appearance.

P009_S0019.reason: The surrounding_context original formula uses a denominator of '2·n_ITO·(λ2-λ1)', while the perturbed version changes it to '4·n_ITO·(λ2-λ1)', which conflicts with the standard thin-film interference formula for maxima spacing and the original equation shown in surrounding_context.
